name: Novu
version: Platform v3.13.0 observed; JS SDK v3.14.0 also current
license: MIT core with commercial/open-core enterprise layers
what: Notification infrastructure platform for email, SMS, push, in-app, and chat notifications with workflows, providers, and templates.
solves: Centralized transactional notification orchestration, provider abstraction, templates, preference handling, and event-driven workflow logic.
users: Product teams and platform teams that want one notification layer rather than bespoke channel logic in every application.
fit: Very high fit. Hylono already uses Novu, and it aligns with booking events, checkout updates, rental lifecycle messaging, service reminders, support notifications, and internal ops alerts.
runtime: API/service layer with worker processes, Redis, MongoDB, storage, provider connectors, and workflow/template management.
components: API, dashboard, workers, Redis, MongoDB, outbound channel providers, templates, and webhooks.
deploy: Self-host via official guides on VMs/containers; production deployments need Redis, MongoDB, storage, secrets, and provider configuration.
deps: MongoDB, Redis, storage, outbound providers, TLS/reverse proxy, and worker scaling.
datastores: MongoDB plus Redis; object/file storage depending on feature use.
auth: API keys, dashboard auth, workspace/user controls, and provider credentials.
apis: REST APIs, SDKs, webhooks, event triggers, templates, and provider integrations.
security: Notification payloads can contain PII and order/service context. Keep provider creds in a secrets system, scope API keys, and minimize sensitive data in templates.
setup_path: Start from official self-host overview, validate required MongoDB/Redis/storage dependencies, and test outbound providers end-to-end before production use.
env: Mongo URL, Redis URL, storage config, API keys, JWT/app secrets, provider credentials, and base URLs.
persistence: Back up MongoDB, preserve template/workflow definitions, and version control IaC for deployment settings.
operations: Watch queue backlogs, provider failures, webhook retries, payload drift, and version compatibility across API/dashboard/workers.
failure_points: Assuming provider abstraction removes deliverability work, under-sizing worker/queue capacity, and storing too much sensitive context in notification payloads.
integration: Direct fit with Next.js API routes, Stripe events, rental flows, Cal.com bookings, CRM updates, n8n workflows, and operational alerting.
verify: Define which notifications stay in Novu versus Resend-only direct sends, and whether customer preferences live in Novu, the site database, or both.
risk: Moderate. High leverage, but notification systems fail noisily if payload ownership, templates, or provider ops are weak.
gdpr: Contains user contact details and event context. Respect consent/preferences, minimize sensitive content, and prefer EU-hosted supporting infrastructure where possible.
overlap: Overlaps with direct Resend sends, basic n8n notifications, Listmonk campaigns, and Mautic journeys, but Novu should remain the transactional notification backbone.
codex_read_first: Read the self-host overview, API/webhook references, and current Hylono messaging flows before adding new templates or events.
codex_best_path: Use Novu as the event-driven transactional layer and keep channel provider credentials outside application code. Feed it from site events and orchestration workflows.
codex_never_assume: Never assume template variables, provider support, or webhook/event semantics without testing on the current platform version.

assets.name: Webhook docs
assets.notes: Useful for event and workflow integration.
